---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# variogramApp

<!-- badges: start -->
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/olatunjijohnson/variogramApp/workflows/R-CMD-check/badge.svg)](https://github.com/olatunjijohnson/variogramApp/actions)
<!-- badges: end -->

An interactive Shiny app for teaching and exploring **variograms** and
**Gaussian random fields**. Designed for statistics courses and workshops.

---

## Try it online

> **Hosted app:** *(coming soon — deploy your own copy with the instructions below)*

Once hosted, students can access it instantly from any browser — no R
installation needed. Share the URL or print a QR code for the classroom.

---

## What the app does

- Choose from **12 covariance models** (Matérn, Exponential, Gaussian,
  Spherical, and more) and see the formula update in real time
- Adjust **variance** (σ²), **scale** (φ), **nugget** (τ²) and
  **smoothness** (κ) with immediate visual feedback
- Simulate Gaussian random fields in **1D** (time series) or **2D** (spatial map)
- Compare the **empirical semivariogram** from a random or inhibitory sample
  against the **theoretical curve**
- Toggle **Monte Carlo permutation envelopes** for a visual significance test
- Show **bin counts** on the variogram plot
- **Download** the variogram plot as a PNG

---

## Installation

### Option A — install the package (recommended for repeated use)

```r
# install.packages("remotes")   # lighter than devtools
remotes::install_github("olatunjijohnson/variogramApp")
```

Then launch the app with:

```r
library(variogramApp)
run_variog_app()
```

### Option B — run directly without installing

This is the quickest option for a computer lab where students already have R
and the dependencies installed:

```r
# install.packages(c("shiny", "geoR", "ggplot2", "dplyr"))
shiny::runGitHub(
  repo     = "variogramApp",
  username = "olatunjijohnson",
  subdir   = "inst/variogramApp"
)
```

---

## Deploy your own hosted copy (for instructors)

Hosting on [shinyapps.io](https://www.shinyapps.io) gives students
**zero-install access** via a URL or QR code.

### 1. Create a free account

Sign up at <https://www.shinyapps.io> (the free tier allows up to 5 apps
and 25 active hours/month — enough for a class).

### 2. Install rsconnect

```r
install.packages("rsconnect")
```

### 3. Connect your account

Copy your token from the shinyapps.io dashboard
(**Account → Tokens → Show**) and run:

```r
rsconnect::setAccountInfo(
  name   = "YOUR_ACCOUNT_NAME",
  token  = "YOUR_TOKEN",
  secret = "YOUR_SECRET"
)
```

### 4. Deploy

```r
rsconnect::deployApp(
  appDir  = system.file("variogramApp", package = "variogramApp"),
  appName = "variogramApp"
)
```

Your app will be live at `https://YOUR_ACCOUNT_NAME.shinyapps.io/variogramApp`.

---

## Generate a QR code for the classroom

Once you have your hosted URL, generate a QR code students can scan:

```r
# install.packages("qrcode")
library(qrcode)
qr <- qr_code("https://YOUR_ACCOUNT_NAME.shinyapps.io/variogramApp")
plot(qr)

# Save as PNG to include in slides or a handout
png("variogramApp_qr.png", width = 300, height = 300)
plot(qr)
dev.off()
```

---

## Dependencies

The app requires the following R packages (installed automatically when
you use `remotes::install_github`):

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `shiny` | Web application framework |
| `geoR` | Geostatistical functions and random field simulation |
| `ggplot2` | Plotting |
| `dplyr` | Data manipulation |

---

## Citation

If you use this app in teaching or research, please cite:

> Johnson, O. (*2021*). *variogramApp: An interactive Shiny application for
> exploring variograms and Gaussian random fields.*
> <https://github.com/olatunjijohnson/variogramApp>
